req.title: Read patient (consent)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-102 mandates three distinct test axes: (1) a clinician assigned to a patient can read that patient (EP positive partition), (2) a clinician NOT assigned is denied with 403 (NEG / DT), and (3) consent revocation timing — specifically reading immediately after revocation vs. after a grace window — exercises a state transition boundary (STC + BVA). An audit record must exist after every permitted read. Tests are structured around a lightweight requests-based fixture that injects a Bearer token; patient IDs and assignment states are seeded via helper fixtures so each test is idempotent.

tests.name: test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: Patient resource exists with known id
Clinician is assigned to the patient in the care-team roster
actions: Obtain a valid Bearer token for the assigned clinician via <get_token>
expected: Response body id matches requested patient_id
At least one AuditEvent record exists for this read operation
trace: R-HLT-102: clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_assigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_200_and_audit(
    assigned_clinician_token,  # <fixture>
    assigned_patient_id,       # <fixture>
    assigned_clinician_id,     # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,             # <fixture>
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Patient"
    assert body.get("id") == assigned_patient_id

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": assigned_patient_id, "agent": assigned_clinician_id},
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_body = audit_resp.json()
    assert audit_body.get("total", 0) >= 1, "Audit record missing after permitted read"

tests.name: test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Patient resource exists with known id
Clinician is NOT assigned to the patient in the care-team roster
actions: Obtain a valid Bearer token for the unassigned clinician via <get_token>
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or equivalent error payload
No patient resource data exposed in the response body
trace: R-HLT-102: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unassigned_clinician_reads_patient_returns_403(
    unassigned_clinician_token,  # <fixture>
    unassigned_patient_id,       # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,               # <fixture>
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {unassigned_clinician_token}"}

    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers=headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403

    body = resp.json()
    # Must not leak patient data; resourceType should not be 'Patient'
    assert body.get("resourceType") != "Patient", (
        "Patient data must not be returned on 403"
    )
    # FHIR servers return OperationOutcome for errors
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_read_patient_immediately_after_consent_revocation_is_denied
preconditions: Patient resource exists with known id
Clinician is assigned to the patient in the care-team roster
Patient consent is currently ACTIVE (pre-condition state)
actions: Obtain a valid Bearer token for the assigned clinician via <get_token>
Confirm read succeeds while consent is active (HTTP GET /Patient/{id})
Revoke patient consent via <revoke_consent> helper (transitions consent to INACTIVE)
Immediately (within the same second) retry HTTP GET /Patient/{id} with same token
Verify consent state recorded as INACTIVE via <get_consent_state>
expected: Second read (consent INACTIVE, T+0s) returns HTTP 403
Consent state reported as INACTIVE confirms the transition occurred before the second request
trace: R-HLT-102: consent state at the time of the operation must be tested
STC: covers ACTIVE->INACTIVE consent state transition boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_read_patient_immediately_after_consent_revocation_is_denied(
    assigned_clinician_token,   # <fixture>
    consented_patient_id,       # <fixture> patient starts with active consent
    fhir_base_url,              # <fixture>
    revoke_consent,             # <helper_fn>(patient_id) -> None
    get_consent_state,          # <helper_fn>(patient_id) -> str
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {assigned_clinician_token}"}
    url = f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{consented_patient_id}"

    # State: ACTIVE — read must succeed
    pre_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 200, "Baseline read with active consent failed"

    # Transition consent to INACTIVE
    revoke_consent(consented_patient_id)
    assert get_consent_state(consented_patient_id) == "INACTIVE"

    # Boundary: read at T+0 after revocation must be denied
    post_resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=10)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 403, (
        "Read must be denied immediately after consent revocation (T+0 boundary)"
    )
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"

tests.name: test_decision_table_role_and_assignment_combinations
preconditions: Multiple accounts exist: admin (role:admin), nurse (role:nurse), assigned clinician (role:clinician, assigned), unassigned clinician (role:clinician, not assigned)
Patient resource exists with known id
actions: For each (token, expected_status) pair in the decision table, HTTP GET /Patient/{id}
trace: R-HLT-102: A clinician shall read; implies non-clinician roles are denied
R-HLT-102: reading outside their assignment shall be denied
DT: exhausts role x assignment decision conditions
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_decision_table_role_and_assignment_combinations(
    assigned_clinician_token,    # <fixture>
    unassigned_clinician_token,  # <fixture>
    admin_token,                 # <fixture>
    nurse_token,                 # <fixture>
    shared_patient_id,           # <fixture> patient assigned only to clinician
    fhir_base_url,               # <fixture>
):
    url = f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{shared_patient_id}"

    decision_table = [
        ("assigned_clinician",   assigned_clinician_token,   200),
        ("unassigned_clinician", unassigned_clinician_token, 403),
        ("admin",               admin_token,                403),
        ("nurse",               nurse_token,                403),
    ]

    failures = []
    for label, token, expected_status in decision_table:
        resp = requests.get(
            url,
            headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
            timeout=10,
        )
        if resp.status_code != expected_status:
            failures.append(
                f"{label}: expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
            )

    assert not failures, "Decision table violations: " + "; ".join(failures)
